noSubtitles: Back to your local model peasant
But that's okay, because everything is running smoothly..right?

lastDecision.rationale: The post text is only a link and provides no explicit indication that any Codex or ChatGPT limit/reset occurred.

recentEvaluations.rationale: The post text is only a link and provides no explicit indication that any Codex or ChatGPT limit/reset occurred.
The tweet explicitly says Codex usage limits were reset across all plans and users can build unlimited things now.
recentEvaluations.tweetText:
Hello. We have reset Codex usage limits across all plans to let everyone experiment with the magnificent plugins we just launched, and because it had been a while! 

You can just build unlimited things with Codex. Have fun!

recentEvaluations.rationale: This is a conversational reply expressing enthusiasm and does not mention limits, caps, resets, or availability.
The tweet is a joke/comment about the Codex team using Codex and says nothing about limits, caps, or usage resetting.
The tweet discusses a Codex-related automation/project and says nothing about limits, caps, availability, or a reset.
recentEvaluations.tweetText: A fun one: I combined it with a skill and automation to physically print a one-page update on public discourse around Codex that I can read together with my morning coffee.

recentEvaluations.rationale: The tweet is about Codex plugins/integrations and account access, not about rate limits, caps, or usage resetting.
recentEvaluations.tweetText:
Boom. Plugins! Codex now has access to most things it couldn’t reach before and all super easy behind the same with you use for your ChatGPT account.

I use this for *everything*, from managing my calendar, triaging incoming bugs, keeping up to date with what everyone is doing at the company and so much more
